rag_rules: Address AGREE syntax errors (e.g., missing semicolons) directly in the contract text.
Focus repairs on the component type annex for assumptions and guarantees.
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the AADL model.
generic_rules: Repairs should be precise and target the specific diagnostic location.
Avoid introducing new AADL elements during repair; focus on correcting existing contracts.
Prioritize using visible AADL features and declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions within the annex.
Range checks should use `value_in >= lower_limit and value_in <= upper_limit` pattern.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types.
Avoid using implementation-level details (subcomponent paths) in component type contracts.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean values to integers for comparisons.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features)
AADL scope rules for component vs. implementation annexes
